steps.title: Set up vertex form using the maximum
steps.content:
The object reaches its maximum height of 185 ft at $t = 2$ s, so the vertex is $(2, 185)$. Vertex form:
$$f(t) = a(t - 2)^2 + 185$$

steps.title: Solve for the leading coefficient using the initial condition
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$f(t) = -16(t+185)^2 - 2$': Places the vertex at $(-185, -2)$, which is completely wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — '$f(t) = -16(t-185)^2 + 2$': Places the vertex at $(185, 2)$; the maximum height would be only 2 ft, not 185. ✗
  • Choice C — '$f(t) = -16(t+2)^2 - 185$': Places the vertex at $(-2, -185)$; the maximum height would be $-185$ ft, which is impossible. ✗
  • Choice D — '$f(t) = -16(t-2)^2 + 185$': Vertex at $(2, 185)$, initial height $f(0) = 121$. Both conditions satisfied. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
